UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Stores additional profile information for users authenticated via an external system, such as contact details and user roles.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity, corresponding to the external authentication system's user ID.

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

address.description: The primary mailing or physical address of the user.

role.description: The role of the user within the system (e.g., 'customer', 'admin', 'staff').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

PreAlert.title: PreAlert
PreAlert.description: Represents a pre-alert request submitted by a user, typically with an image of a receipt, to inform about an incoming package.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PreAlert entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who submitted this pre-alert. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N PreAlert)

status.description: The current status of the pre-alert (e.g., 'pending', 'approved', 'rejected', 'action_required').

receiptImageUri.description: URI to the uploaded image of the receipt or delivery slip.

suggestedDeliveryAddress.description: The delivery address suggested by the AI or provided by the user based on the receipt.

itemDescription.description: A brief description of the item(s) in the pre-alert.

trackingNumber.description: The tracking number provided by the seller/sender, if available.

estimatedValue.description: The estimated monetary value of the items.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the pre-alert was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the pre-alert was last updated.

Shipment.title: Shipment
Shipment.description: Represents a single shipping consignment, tracking its journey from origin to destination.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Shipment entity.

trackingNumber.description: The unique tracking number assigned to this shipment.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns or requested this shipment. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Shipment)

preAlertId.description: Optional reference to the PreAlert that initiated this shipment. (Relationship: PreAlert 1:1 Shipment)

currentStatus.description: The current status of the shipment (e.g., 'received', 'in_transit', 'out_for_delivery', 'delivered', 'cancelled').

originAddress.description: The address where the shipment originated.

destinationAddress.description: The final delivery address for the shipment.

recipientName.description: The name of the person receiving the shipment.

recipientContact.description: The contact information (e.g., phone number) of the recipient.

packageWeight.description: The weight of the package in a standard unit (e.g., kg or lbs).

estimatedDeliveryDate.description: The estimated date and time of delivery.

actualDeliveryDate.description: The actual date and time of delivery.

carrier.description: The name of the external carrier handling the shipment (if any).

flightManifestId.description: Optional reference to the FlightManifest this shipment is part of. (Relationship: FlightManifest 1:N Shipment)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the shipment record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the shipment record was last updated.

ShipmentStatusLog.title: ShipmentStatusLog
ShipmentStatusLog.description: Records individual status updates and events for a particular shipment, providing a historical trail.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ShipmentStatusLog entity.

shipmentId.description: Reference to the Shipment this status update belongs to. (Relationship: Shipment 1:N ShipmentStatusLog)

status.description: The specific status or event being logged (e.g., 'package_received', 'departed_warehouse', 'arrived_destination_country', 'out_for_delivery', 'delivered').

location.description: The geographical location where the status update occurred.

timestamp.description: The date and time when this status update occurred.

description.description: An optional detailed description of the status update.

FlightManifest.title: FlightManifest
FlightManifest.description: Represents a document or record detailing the cargo loaded onto a specific flight for transport.

id.description: Unique identifier for the FlightManifest entity.

manifestNumber.description: A unique number for this flight manifest.

departureAirportCode.description: The IATA code of the departure airport.

arrivalAirportCode.description: The IATA code of the arrival airport.

scheduledDepartureTime.description: The scheduled date and time of departure.

actualDepartureTime.description: The actual date and time of departure.

scheduledArrivalTime.description: The scheduled date and time of arrival.

actualArrivalTime.description: The actual date and time of arrival.

status.description: The current status of the manifest or flight (e.g., 'planned', 'departed', 'arrived', 'cancelled').

documentUri.description: URI to the digital copy of the flight manifest document.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the flight manifest record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the flight manifest record was last updated.

Revenue.title: Revenue
Revenue.description: Records all income generated by the shipping service.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Revenue entity.

shipmentId.description: Optional reference to the Shipment associated with this revenue. (Relationship: Shipment 1:N Revenue)

amount.description: The monetary amount of the revenue.

currency.description: The currency of the revenue amount (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR').

description.description: A description of the revenue source (e.g., 'Shipping Fee', 'Customs Duty', 'Storage Fee').

transactionDate.description: The date and time when the revenue was recorded or generated.

type.description: The category or type of revenue (e.g., 'Shipping', 'Service', 'Other').

collectedByUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile (staff/admin) who recorded or processed this revenue. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Revenue)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the revenue record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the revenue record was last updated.

Expense.title: Expense
Expense.description: Records all expenditures incurred by the shipping service.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Expense entity.

associatedShipmentId.description: Optional reference to a Shipment if the expense is directly tied to it. (Relationship: Shipment 1:N Expense)

amount.description: The monetary amount of the expense.

currency.description: The currency of the expense amount (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR').

description.description: A description of the expense (e.g., 'Fuel Cost', 'Warehouse Rent', 'Staff Salary', 'Customs Clearance Fee').

transactionDate.description: The date and time when the expense was incurred or recorded.

type.description: The category or type of expense (e.g., 'Operating', 'Logistics', 'Administrative', 'Customs').

recordedByUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile (staff/admin) who recorded this expense. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Expense)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the expense record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the expense record was last updated.

definition.description: Stores additional profile information for users, linked directly to their Firebase Auth UID. Each document's 'id' field matches the `{userId}` path parameter. Accessible by the user themselves or by administrative roles.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, corresponding to their Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: User-specific pre-alert requests. Each document includes a denormalized 'ownerId' field (matching `{userId}`) for authorization independence. This hierarchical structure enables secure and efficient 'list' operations for users to retrieve only their own pre-alerts.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this pre-alert.

params.name: preAlertId
params.description: The unique identifier for the pre-alert.

definition.description: User-specific shipment records. Each document includes a denormalized 'ownerId' field (matching `{userId}`) for authorization independence. This enables secure 'list' operations for users to retrieve only their own shipments.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this shipment.

params.name: shipmentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the shipment.

definition.description: Detailed status logs for a specific user's shipment. Each document includes a denormalized 'ownerId' field (matching `{userId}`) for authorization independence, allowing direct 'list' and 'read' access for the user without requiring a 'get()' on the parent shipment.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent shipment.

params.name: shipmentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent shipment.

params.name: logId
params.description: The unique identifier for the status log entry.

definition.description: Global collection for managing flight manifests, primarily by staff/admin. Access for regular users is typically indirect (details denormalized into shipments) or through specific read permissions for relevant manifest IDs, preventing broad 'list' access for non-privileged users.

params.name: flightManifestId
params.description: The unique identifier for the flight manifest.

definition.description: Financial revenue records, exclusively accessible by staff/admin roles. Includes 'collectedByUserId' for auditing which privileged user recorded the revenue.

params.name: revenueId
params.description: The unique identifier for the revenue record.

definition.description: Financial expense records, exclusively accessible by staff/admin roles. Includes 'recordedByUserId' for auditing which privileged user recorded the expense.

params.name: expenseId
params.description: The unique identifier for the expense record.

definition.description: A collection used for Database Access Control. The mere existence of a document at `/admin_roles/$(request.auth.uid)` grants administrative privileges in security rules, embodying 'existence over content' for authorization independence. This 'AdminRole' entity is conceptual and its schema is typically minimal, often just the 'id' field.

params.name: adminUid
params.description: The unique identifier (UID) of a user designated as an administrator.

definition.description: A collection used for Database Access Control. The mere existence of a document at `/staff_roles/$(request.auth.uid)` grants staff privileges in security rules, embodying 'existence over content' for authorization independence. This 'StaffRole' entity is conceptual and its schema is typically minimal, often just the 'id' field.

params.name: staffUid
params.description: The unique identifier (UID) of a user designated as a staff member.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure is designed to adhere strictly to the core principles of Authorization Independence, Clarity of Intent, DBAC, and QAPs, ensuring a secure, scalable, and debuggable backend for SwiftRoute. The primary strategy involves a hierarchical, user-centric data model for user-owned information and segregated top-level collections for global, administrative data.

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
To eliminate hierarchical authorization dependencies and avoid `get()` calls in security rules, crucial authorization context is denormalized. For user-specific data entities such as `PreAlert`, `Shipment`, and `ShipmentStatusLog`, each document will explicitly contain an `ownerId` field. This `ownerId` will consistently match the `userId` segment in the document's path (`/users/{userId}/...`) and `request.auth.uid`. This allows security rules to directly compare `request.auth.uid` with the document's own fields and its path, ensuring atomic authorization checks without needing to fetch parent documents.

**Secure `list` Operations (QAPs) through Structural Segregation:**

The structure addresses the "list" operation security error (`FirebaseError: Missing or insufficient permissions: ... /pre_alerts`) by enforcing Queriable Authorization Policies (QAPs). Instead of a flat collection where users might attempt to `list` all pre-alerts and rely on rules to 'filter' (`.where('userId', '==', request.auth.uid)`), user-specific data is placed under a user's direct path:
*   `PreAlerts` are stored in `/users/{userId}/pre_alerts/{preAlertId}`.
*   `Shipments` are stored in `/users/{userId}/shipments/{shipmentId}`.
*   `ShipmentStatusLogs` are stored in `/users/{userId}/shipments/{shipmentId}/status_logs/{logId}`.

This structure ensures that any `list` request for these collections by an authenticated user **must** include their `uid` in the path (e.g., `/users/$(request.auth.uid)/pre_alerts`). Security rules can then simply verify that the `userId` in the path matches `request.auth.uid`, effectively scoping the `list` operation to only the requesting user's data. This prevents broad, insecure `list` queries and eliminates the need for rules to act as filters.

**DBAC (Database Access Control) and Role Management:**
User roles (`admin`, `staff`, `customer`) are managed using dedicated, top-level collections for 'existence over content' checks, completely avoiding custom claims or `get()` calls on `UserProfile` documents for role-based access to other entities.
*   `/admin_roles/{adminUid}`: An empty document at this path signifies an administrator.
*   `/staff_roles/{staffUid}`: An empty document at this path signifies a staff member.
Security rules can then use `exists(/admin_roles/$(request.auth.uid))` or `exists(/staff_roles/$(request.auth.uid))` for efficient, independent role checks. The `UserProfile.role` field is maintained for application-level logic and reporting, not for Firestore security rules.

**Structural Segregation for Homogeneous Security:**

*   **User-Specific Data:** Collections like `pre_alerts`, `shipments`, and `status_logs` within a user's path (`/users/{userId}/...`) have a homogeneous security posture: primarily owned and accessed by that specific user, with potential read access for authenticated staff/admins.
*   **Administrative/Global Data:** Collections such as `flight_manifests`, `revenue`, and `expenses` are at the root level (`/flight_manifests`, `/revenue`, `/expenses`). These collections have a homogeneous security posture, restricted primarily to staff and administrators for management, with minimal or no direct `list` access for regular users. This prevents unauthorized users from broadly querying sensitive operational data.

**Data Clarity and Predictability:**
All paths use descriptive wildcards (e.g., `{userId}`, `{shipmentId}`). Entities consistently use a single `status` field for state management and include `createdAt` and `updatedAt` for auditing. Authorization fields like `ownerId` are standardized.

This design ensures that security rules are simple, explicit, and easy to audit, directly addressing the principles for a robust Firebase backend.
